description_long: This bonsai features a thick, mature trunk with a textured grayish-brown bark, rising from a base surrounded by exposed roots and a patch of earth. The roots are prominent, spreading outward and partially above the soil, giving the tree a sense of age and stability. The ground around the trunk is covered with a mix of green grass, small plants, and scattered leaves, suggesting a natural outdoor environment. The trunk itself is mostly straight with subtle undulations, and there are no visible branches or dense foliage in the immediate view, focusing attention on the trunk and root structure. The soil appears compacted and slightly uneven, bordered by a thin layer of grass. The overall impression is of a robust, well-established bonsai, likely modeled after a species such as Ficus or Elm, with a strong emphasis on trunk and root aesthetics.
description: A robust bonsai with a thick, textured trunk and prominent exposed roots, set in a patch of earth with grass and scattered leaves. The focus is on the trunk and root structure, with minimal visible foliage.
description_view_0: This view highlights the thick trunk and a large root extending forward, with a mix of grass and bare soil at the base.
description_view_1: The trunk appears slightly darker, and the roots are more spread out to the sides, with less grass coverage and more visible soil.
description_view_2: The trunk is straight and the roots fan out symmetrically, with a patch of green grass and some fallen leaves visible.
description_view_3: This angle shows the trunk with subtle bark markings and a dense patch of grass at the base, with roots extending diagonally.
attribute_pot_type: No visible pot; the bonsai appears to be planted directly in a natural patch of earth with grass and soil.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is mostly straight with subtle undulations, resembling a formal upright style with minor natural bends.
attribute_branch_arrangement: No visible branches; the focus is on the trunk and root structure, with any branches likely pruned or out of view.
attribute_leaf_density: Sparse, with minimal foliage visible and most of the visual emphasis on the trunk and roots.
